title: Requirements for Renewal of License under 2002 POEA Rules on Overseas Employment of OFWs
content:
1. Duly accomplished POEA-LRO renewal application form.
2. Renewed or revalidated surety bond in the amount of P100,000.00 valid for four years together with official receipt.
3. Renewed escrow agreement in the amount of P1,000,000.00 with a commercial bank with confirmation of escrow deposit.
4. Audited financial statements for the past two years with verified corporate or individual tax returns or individual income tax returns of the single proprietor or partner. If the equity of the agency is below the minimum capitalization requirement, the agency shall be given thirty (30) days from release of the renewed license to submit proof(s) of capital infusion; otherwise, the license shall be suspended until compliance.
5. NBI clearance issued not earlier than six (6) months from date of filing of application and clearance from the POEA Anti-Illegal Recruitment Branch for all Directors, officers, and employees involved in recruitment and placement. For directors or partners with foreign nationality, clearance from their country of origin may be submitted in place of the NBI clearance.
6. Other requirements as may be imposed by the Administration.
